Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 347 upvotes, 62 comments
  2. 15 Years of Forking 246 upvotes, 51 comments
  3. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 273 upvotes, 65 comments
  4. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 46 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 1041 upvotes, 303 comments
  6. Hardware Image Compression 47 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 32 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 97 upvotes, 26 comments
  9. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 610 upvotes, 227 comments
  10. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 90 upvotes, 26 comments
  11. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 53 upvotes, 15 comments
  12. How to Turn Anything into a Router 117 upvotes, 42 comments
  13. VHDL's Crown Jewel 96 upvotes, 35 comments
  14. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 338 upvotes, 166 comments
  15. I use excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 168 upvotes, 77 comments
  16. How Reverse Game Theory Could Solve the Housing Shortage 12 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 816 upvotes, 528 comments
  18. How A Spartan Revolutionized Baseball 22 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 73 upvotes, 36 comments
  20. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 40 upvotes, 19 comments
  21. Ghostmoon.app – The Swiss Army Knife for your macOS menu bar 110 upvotes, 84 comments
  22. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 19 upvotes, 9 comments
  23. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 132 upvotes, 114 comments
  24. Coding agents could make free software matter again 240 upvotes, 237 comments
  25. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 276 upvotes, 288 comments
  26. Eclipse GlassFish: This Isn't Your Father's GlassFish 40 upvotes, 35 comments
  27. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 291 upvotes, 342 comments
  28. How the AI Bubble Bursts 256 upvotes, 304 comments
  29. The First Video Game Was Just a Box in the Corner of a Bar 26 upvotes, 21 comments
  30. The coming PLG to SLG apocalypse 3 upvotes, 1 comments
